description:
You are an automation specialist who needs to perform a very quick
    security-oriented source-code scan that will be consumed by another
    pipeline stage.  
    A tiny demo code-base is already present on the system at

        /home/user/security_scan/sample_code/

    Goal
    ----
    1. Search every “*.py” file inside the directory above (include all
       sub-directories, if any) for the literal string

           eval(

       The match is case-sensitive and must include the opening parenthesis.
    2. Create a text file named

           /home/user/security_scan/scan_report.txt

       that lists every match in *exactly* the format described below.

    Required report format
    ----------------------
    • First line *must* be the exact header (no leading/trailing blanks):

          INSECURE FUNCTION SCAN REPORT

    • Each subsequent line (one per match) must follow this template
      precisely:

          <filename>:<line_number>:<trimmed_line_contents>

      where  
      – <filename> is the basename of the file that contains the match  
      – <line_number> is the 1-based line number where the match occurs  
      – <trimmed_line_contents> is the full line text with all leading
        spaces removed (do not append or remove other characters).

    • After the last match line, add a final summary line:

          TOTAL OCCURRENCES: <N>

      where <N> is the total count of matching lines found.

    • Every line in the report, including the last one, must be terminated
      with a single newline “\n”.

    Constraints
    -----------
    • Do **not** search outside /home/user/security_scan/sample_code/.  
    • Use only standard GNU/Linux user-space tools already present in a
      minimal distribution (e.g., grep, awk, sed, find, etc.).  
    • The task should require no more than 2–3 commands to complete.

    Deliverable
    -----------
    The file /home/user/security_scan/scan_report.txt exactly matching the
    format above.  Automated tests will compare your file byte-for-byte, so
    pay close attention to spacing, colons, and newlines.
truth:
Initial files and their exact contents (each line ends with “\n”):

    1. /home/user/security_scan/sample_code/app.py
       ------------------------------------------------
       # Sample app\n
       user_input = input("Enter expression: ")\n
       result = eval(user_input)  # insecure\n
       print(result)\n
       data = "print('hello')"\n
       eval(data)\n

    2. /home/user/security_scan/sample_code/utils.py
       ------------------------------------------------
       def calculate(expr):\n
           return eval(expr)  # insecure\n

    Expected final report (/home/user/security_scan/scan_report.txt):
       INSECURE FUNCTION SCAN REPORT\n
       app.py:3:result = eval(user_input)  # insecure\n
       app.py:6:eval(data)\n
       utils.py:2:return eval(expr)  # insecure\n
       TOTAL OCCURRENCES: 3\n
